originalTitle: Kenya minister found in contempt of court over US-backed Ebola centre
title: A Big Story About Rules and Health in Kenya!
content:
Hello, My Little Friends!

Hi everyone! It's your kid-friendly news reporter here, bringing you super interesting stories from our amazing continent, Africa! Today, we have a story from a beautiful country called Kenya. It's about big plans, big buildings, and even bigger rules! Sometimes, even grown-ups forget to follow rules, just like sometimes we might forget to put our toys away. Let's find out what happened!

A Big Plan for a Special Building

Imagine a place where clever doctors and scientists can learn all about different sicknesses. This helps them find ways to keep everyone healthy and strong! In Kenya, there was a plan to build a very special place like this. It was going to be a centre to help people learn about a sickness called Ebola. Money to build it was going to come from a country far away called America, also known as the USA. **Did you know?** Kenya is a country in East Africa. It has amazing animals like lions and elephants, and beautiful beaches!

Who is Mr. Aden Duale?

Now, in every country, there are people who help run things. They are called ministers. They are like very important helpers for the whole country. Our story is about a minister named Mr. Aden Duale. He has a very big job in Kenya's government. He helps make big decisions for the country. He is like a very important leader who helps make things happen.

What is a High Court and a Judge?

When there are rules, sometimes people need help to make sure everyone follows them. That's where judges come in! A judge is like a super-smart grown-up who helps make sure everything is fair. They work in a place called a High Court. Think of the High Court as a very important classroom where judges help solve big problems and make sure rules are followed by everyone, even very important people like ministers! **Did you know?** Judges wear special robes, just like some teachers wear a special uniform! Their job is to be fair to everyone, no matter who they are.

What is Ebola? Don't Worry!

Now, you might hear the word Ebola. Don't worry, it's a very rare sickness. It's like a special germ that can make people very unwell. But it's not something we see every day, and doctors know a lot about how to help people who get it. This special building was going to be a safe place for smart people to study germs like Ebola. By studying them, they can find ways to stop them from making people sick in the future. It's all about keeping us safe and healthy! **Did you know?** There are many clever doctors and nurses in Africa who work super hard every day to keep everyone healthy and strong!

Why Build a Centre to Study Sickness?

So, why would Kenya want a special building to study Ebola and other germs? Well, imagine you have a puzzle. If you want to solve it, you need a special place to work on it, right? This centre would be like that special place. Doctors and scientists could safely study the puzzle of these sicknesses. They could learn how to make medicines and how to stop the germs from spreading. It's a very important job! It helps protect our families and communities. **Did you know?** Scientists use super powerful microscopes to see tiny germs that are too small for our eyes to see! It's like magic, but it's science!

Why Did Some People Have Questions?

Even though the idea of learning about sicknesses is good, sometimes people have different ideas about where to build things. Or they might have questions about how it will be built. For this special Ebola centre, some people in Kenya had questions. They wanted to make sure everything was super safe for everyone living nearby. It's good to ask questions when you are planning big things, right? Just like you might ask your parents questions about a new game or a new pet!

A Rule from the Judge

Because some people had questions and worries, the High Court judge stepped in. The judge looked at everything and said, "Hold on a minute! Let's pause the building work for now." This was like a special rule or an order. The judge wanted everyone to stop building until all the questions were answered and everyone felt safe and happy. It's very important to listen to what the judge says, because they are there to make things fair for everyone. This rule is called a court order. **Did you know?** Following rules helps keep everyone safe and happy, whether it's at school, at home, or in a whole country! Rules help us all live together nicely.

Did Mr. Duale Listen to the Judge?

Uh oh. This is where our story gets a little tricky. The judge gave a clear order: "Stop building!" But it seems Mr. Aden Duale, our important minister, didn't stop the building work. He continued with the plan to build the centre. This is what we call "contempt of court." It means he didn't listen to the judge's very important rule. It's like if your teacher tells you to stop running in the classroom, but you keep running. What do you think happens then?

What Happens When You Don't Listen to a Judge?

When someone doesn't listen to a judge's order, it's a big deal. The judge wants to know why. So, Mr. Duale now has to go back to the High Court. He needs to explain why he didn't follow the rule. The judge wants to hear his side of the story. This is how rules work. Everyone, no matter how important they are, must follow the rules that keep a country fair and safe. It's a reminder that rules are for everyone! **Did you know?** Even presidents and prime ministers have to follow the laws and rules of their country! Nobody is above the law.

Learning Together for a Healthy Future!

This story teaches us that even when people have good intentions, like building a centre to fight sickness, it's super important to listen to everyone and follow the rules. Especially rules from a judge! It helps make sure that big projects are done in the best and safest way for all the wonderful people of Kenya. Let's hope that everyone can work together to make sure Kenya stays healthy and strong, and that rules are always followed!
summary: An important leader in Kenya didn't follow a judge's rule about building a special health centre, and now he needs to explain why.
whyMatters: This story reminds us that everyone, even important leaders, must follow rules to keep things fair and safe for everyone in the country. It's important for big projects to be built safely and with everyone's questions answered.

originalTitle: Twins marry twins in joyous Nigerian joint wedding
title: Twins Marry Twins! A Double Celebration in Nigeria!
content:
Hello My Friends!

Hi there, amazing kids! It's your friendly news reporter here, bringing you super exciting news from our wonderful continent, Africa! Are you ready for a story that will make you smile? It's about love, happiness, and… wait for it… TWINS! Yes, twins marrying twins! How cool is that?

What Are Twins?

Do you know what twins are? They are two babies born at the same time to the same mommy! Sometimes twins look exactly, exactly alike. You might not be able to tell them apart! We call them identical twins. Other times, twins look a bit different, just like regular brothers and sisters, but they were born on the same day. Being a twin is very special! You always have a best friend with you from day one.

A Super Special Day in Nigeria!

This amazing story comes from a big, beautiful country in Africa called Nigeria. Nigeria is a land of vibrant colours, delicious food, and lots of friendly people. And it was there that something truly magical happened. A very special wedding day! Not just one wedding, but a joint wedding! That means two weddings happening at the same big party!

Meet the Twin Brothers!

Let me introduce you to our heroes! Their names are Taiwo and Kehinde. They are brothers, and guess what? They are identical twins! That means they look super much alike. Maybe they even wear the same clothes sometimes! Taiwo and Kehinde grew up together, played together, and shared everything. They were always a team, a dynamic duo!

Meet the Twin Sisters!

Now, for the lovely ladies! Our story doesn't tell us their names, but we know they are also twin sisters! Just like Taiwo and Kehinde, these sisters grew up together. They shared secrets, laughed at jokes, and supported each other every day. Imagine having a sister who looks just like you! It must be so much fun.

How Did They Find Each Other?

This is the super amazing part! Taiwo met one of the twin sisters, and Kehinde met the other twin sister! Imagine that! It's like destiny, or magic, or just very good luck. Maybe they met at a party, or at school, or through friends. They fell in love! Both brothers fell in love with both sisters. And all four of them are twins! It's like a fairy tale come true. They decided they wanted to spend their whole lives together.

A Big Happy Party!

So, what happens when two sets of twins decide to get married? You get a HUGE, happy party! This was a joint wedding, which means the two twin brothers married the two twin sisters on the same day, at the same place. There was music playing, people dancing, and lots of singing. Weddings in Nigeria are always full of joy and celebration. Everyone dressed in their most beautiful clothes. There was probably so much delicious food to eat too! Jollof rice, pounded yam, and yummy stews! Yum!

Why This Is So Amazing!

This story is super special because it's quite rare! It's not every day that you see one twin marry another twin. But for *two* sets of twins to fall in love and get married at the same time? That is truly, truly special! It shows that love can find you in the most surprising ways. Imagine the families! Now they have even more twins in their family. It’s like a double, double celebration!

Did You Know? Fun Facts!

* **Did you know?** Nigeria has the most people of any country in Africa! It's a very big and important country. * **Did you know?** Some studies say that Nigeria has more twins born than almost anywhere else in the world! Maybe that's why this super story happened there! * **Did you know?** In many African cultures, twins are seen as a special blessing. People believe they bring good luck and happiness to families. * **Did you know?** The names Taiwo and Kehinde are very common names for twins in the Yoruba culture in Nigeria! Taiwo is usually the first twin born, and Kehinde is the second. * **Did you know?** Weddings in Africa are often very big, happy events that can last for many hours, sometimes even days! They are a chance for everyone to celebrate together.

Wishing Them Happiness!

So, our twin brothers Taiwo and Kehinde have married their twin sisters. They started their new lives together with a big, joyous celebration. We wish them all the happiness in the world! May their homes always be filled with laughter, love, and double the fun! This story reminds us that love is wonderful, and special things can happen every day, especially here in Africa!
summary: Two twin brothers in Nigeria found and married two twin sisters in a wonderful joint wedding celebration!
whyMatters: This story is special because it shows us that love is amazing and special things can happen, and it celebrates a very rare and happy event in our continent, Africa.

title: Former Kenyan justice minister blocked from entering Uganda, lawyers' body says
content: Martha  Karua is a lawyer representing detained Ugandan opposition leader Kizza Besigye who is on trial for treason.

originalTitle: From retirement to records - another immortal Messi moment
title: From retirement to records - another immortal Messi moment

originalTitle: Norway win five-goal thriller against Senegal to make last 32
title: Norway win five-goal thriller against Senegal to make last 32
content: Erling Haaland scores twice as Norway book their place in the last 32 of the World Cup with a 3-2 victory over Senegal in New Jersey.
